data.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Due to the increasing availability of high-quality genome sequences, pan-genomes are gradually replacing single consensus reference genomes in many bioinformatics pipelines to better capture genetic diversity. Traditional bioinformatics tools using the FM-index face memory limitations with such large genome collections. Recent advancements in run-length compressed indices like Gagie et al.’s r-index and Nishimoto and Tabei’s move structure, alleviate memory constraints but focus primarily on backward search for MEM-finding. Arakawa et al.’s br-index initiates complete approximate pattern matching using bidirectional search in run-length compressed space, but with significant computational overhead due to complex memory access patterns. We introduce b-move, a novel bidirectional extension of the move structure, enabling fast, cache-efficient bidirectional character extensions in run-length compressed space. It achieves bidirectional character extensions up to 8 times faster than the br-index, closing the performance gap with FM-index-based alternatives, while maintaining the br-index’s favorable memory characteristics. For example, all available complete
                  <jats:italic>E. coli</jats:italic>
                  genomes on NCBI’s RefSeq collection can be compiled into a b-move index that fits into the RAM of a typical laptop. Thus, b-move proves practical and scalable for pan-genome indexing and querying. We provide a C++ implementation of b-move, supporting efficient lossless approximate pattern matching including locate functionality, available at
                  <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/biointec/b-move">https://github.com/biointec/b-move</jats:ext-link>
                  under the AGPL-3.0 license.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Funding</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    <jats:italic>Lore Depuydt</jats:italic>
                    : PhD Fellowship FR (1117322N), Research Foundation – Flanders (FWO)
                  </jats:p>
                  <jats:p>
                    <jats:italic>Luca Renders</jats:italic>
                    : PhD Fellowship SB (1SE7822N), Research Foundation – Flanders (FWO)
                  </jats:p>
                  <jats:p>
                    <jats:italic>Travis Gagie</jats:italic>
                    : NSERC Discovery Grant RGPIN-07185-2020 to Travis Gagie and NIH grant R01HG011392 to Ben Langmead
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
data.title: b-move: faster bidirectional character extensions in a run-length compressed index

reference.unstructured: Michael Burrows and David Wheeler . A Block-Sorting Lossless Data Compression Algorithm. Research Report 124, Digital Equipment Corporation Systems Research Center, 130 Lytton Avenue, Palo Alto, California 94301, May 1994.
Julian Seward . bzip2 and libbzip2 - a program and library for data compression. avaliable at http://www.bzip.org, 1996.
